01_initial_request.input_data: landscape planning and design system parameters, climate factors, and evaluation index weights
01_initial_request.user_intent: To improve energy conservation and environmental protection in cold urban landscape design while enhancing livability and sustainability
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Minimization of land use, water consumption, financial allocation, and ecological disturbance based on evaluation index weights

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Urban public landscape design in cold regions faces challenges regarding energy consumption and resource waste. [Gap] Traditional optimization methods cannot meet modern computing requirements for complex engineering structures in landscape planning. [Decision] To achieve energy-efficient landscape design, chose to use Data Modulation Method to perform preprocessing on existing public landscape environment data, expecting to obtain grid data of the existing public landscape pattern as initial data.

parameters.output_format: grid data of the existing public landscape pattern

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Grid data of the existing public landscape pattern established as initial data for algorithm input.

references.relevance: Supports the need for adapting landscape design to regional characteristics and environmental conditions.
Highlights problems in public landscape design in cold cities requiring data-driven solutions.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Energy-saving landscaping involves many links and requires a systematic evaluation. [Gap] Weights given by experience sometimes cannot objectively reflect the actual situation, resulting in distortion of evaluation results. [Decision] To achieve objective weight determination, chose to use Yaahp software to perform consistency test and weight calculation on evaluation indexes, expecting to obtain weight values of each indicator for energy-efficient landscape design.

parameters.method: hierarchical single ranking and total ranking
parameters.target: energy-efficient landscape design for urban parks

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Weight values of each indicator obtained after consistency test passed (CR<0.05).

references.relevance: Supports the systematic literature review approach for ecological urban planning and design evaluation.
Relates to research on design of agricultural leisure landscape in cold regions requiring evaluation indexes.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Traditional optimization methods struggle with complete problems in actual production processes. [Gap] Need an algorithm that can take into account both local and global search for landscape resource allocation. [Decision] To achieve optimization of land resources and energy saving, chose to use Group Intelligence Algorithm (incorporating ACO, PSO, GA) to perform iterative optimization on landscape design parameters, expecting to obtain optimal coefficients and design scheme.

parameters.fitness_function: error sum of squares criterion function
parameters.update_rules: pheromone update, particle position update, genetic crossover/mutation

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Evolutionary mean of 2109.1Hz achieved; algorithm demonstrates superior ability to take into account both local and global search.

references.relevance: Supports the design and application of group intelligence algorithm for smart city contexts.
Supports developing group intelligence optimization algorithms to solve practical engineering optimization problems.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The performance of the proposed algorithm needs to be validated against existing methods. [Gap] It is unclear if the group intelligence algorithm outperforms standard algorithms in this domain. [Decision] To achieve performance validation, chose to use Comparative Analysis to perform simulation comparison against Apriori and SVM algorithms, expecting to obtain optimization percentage results.

parameters.thresholds: different set thresholds of the population operator

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Group intelligence algorithm outperformed another algorithm by 13.45% in terms of average optimization ability.

references.citation: [6] C. Lim and P. P. Maglio, 2018
references.relevance: Supports data-driven understanding of service systems through text mining and comparison.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Simulation Comparison and Optimization Percentage Calculation

optimization_improvement.interpretation: Average optimization ability improvement of group intelligence algorithm over another algorithm.

evolutionary_mean.interpretation: Evolutionary mean value achieved during the algorithm execution.

consistency_ratio.interpretation: Maximum acceptable consistency ratio for weight determination (CR<0.05).

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The initial objective was achieved. The group intelligence algorithm demonstrated superior ability to take into account both local and global search, improving average optimization ability by 13.45%, supporting the construction of energy-efficient gardens as part of an energy-efficient society.
